name: Per Se
description: Thomas Keller's New York interpretation of The French Laundry, perched above Columbus Circle with Central Park views. A nine-course tasting menu of exacting French-American cuisine that has held three Michelin stars for two decades.
dishes: Snake River Farms Calotte de Boeuf
address: 10 Columbus Circle, 4th Floor, New York NY 10019

name: Jungsik
description: Chef Jungsik Yim's groundbreaking New Korean cuisine earned three Michelin stars in 2024, the first Korean restaurant in America to do so. The tasting menu reimagines Korean traditions with French technique in a sleek TriBeCa dining room.

name: Sushi Sho
description: Chef Keiji Nakazawa's intimate eight-seat Hinoki counter earned three Michelin stars within a year of opening, the first sushi restaurant in NYC to reach that level since Masa. Extraordinary edomae technique with pioneering fermentation.

name: Gabriel Kreuther
description: Chef Gabriel Kreuther brings Alsatian warmth to a luxurious Midtown dining room overlooking Bryant Park. Two Michelin stars recognize his masterful French technique and seasonal tasting menus that honor his roots in the Alsace countryside.

name: Daniel
awards: Michelin 1-Star, James Beard Outstanding Chef, NY Times 4-Star
description: Daniel Boulud's flagship has defined Upper East Side fine dining for over three decades. Multicourse French tasting menus showcase impeccable technique in a grand room with coffered ceilings and Bernardaud chandeliers.

name: Torrisi
description: Rich Torrisi's triumphant return to his roots in the landmark Puck Building on Mulberry Street. Imaginative Italian-American fine dining that balances nostalgia and innovation, with tuxedoed waiters and pressed linen tablecloths.

name: COTE Korean Steakhouse
awards: Michelin 1-Star, James Beard Best New Restaurant 2018
description: The world's first Michelin-starred Korean BBQ, blending the conviviality of tabletop grilling with the refinement of a great American steakhouse. USDA Prime beef, 1,200-label wine list, and the iconic Butcher's Feast omakase.

name: Estela
description: Ignacio Mattos' boisterous SoHo alcove above Houston Street where bold, deceptively simple plates become iconic. Over a decade in, Estela remains one of the most influential restaurants in New York, a perfect spot for drinks and inventive small plates.
address: 47 E Houston St, New York NY 10012

name: Semma
awards: Michelin 1-Star, James Beard Best Chef NY 2025, NY Times #1 Restaurant
description: Chef Vijay Kumar's Michelin-starred celebration of regional South Indian cuisine that refuses to pander to Western expectations. Fiery Chettinad dishes, coastal curries, and temple-style vegetarian fare in a cozy Greenwich Village space.

name: Le Pavillon
description: Daniel Boulud's stunning seafood-and-vegetable-focused restaurant in the soaring lobby of One Vanderbilt, across from Grand Central. The 57-foot ceilings, 20-foot olive trees, and masterful French cooking make this one of Midtown's most beautiful dining rooms.

name: Frenchette
awards: Michelin Bib Gourmand, James Beard Outstanding Restaurateur 2025
description: The brasserie that TriBeCa always deserved, from ex-Balthazar chefs Lee Hanson and Riad Nasr. Classic French bistro cooking with a joyful energy that earned the James Beard Outstanding Restaurateur award. Duck frites is non-negotiable.

name: Francie
description: Williamsburg's Michelin-starred neighborhood brasserie from Chef Chris Cipollone. Approachable yet refined cooking -- handmade pastas, pristine shellfish, and the show-stopping Crown Roast of Rohan Duck -- in a warm corner room on Broadway.

name: Oxomoco
description: A Michelin-starred Mexican restaurant in Greenpoint with a gorgeous garden patio and wood-fired kitchen. Creative tacos, expertly charred vegetables, and a mezcal-forward cocktail program make this one of Brooklyn's most exciting restaurants.

name: Yamada
description: Kaiseki master Isao Yamada's intimate 10-seat counter in Chinatown earned a Michelin star and a rare four-star NY Times review within months of opening. A deeply personal expression of seasonality built on decades of craft.

name: Dirt Candy
description: Amanda Cohen's pioneering vegetable-focused tasting menu restaurant proves vegetables can be the star. One of only two Michelin-starred vegetarian restaurants in NYC, with a no-tipping policy and seasonal menus built around single vegetables.

name: Casa Mono
description: Chef Anthony Sasso has kept this intimate Gramercy tapas bar at one Michelin star since 2009. Inspired by Spain's Costa Brava, the tiny space next to Union Square rewards adventurous eaters with bold flavors and an outstanding Spanish wine list.

name: L'Artusi
description: A West Village neighborhood staple for refined yet approachable Italian cooking. The crudo bar, handmade pastas, and specialty cheese program draw loyal regulars, and the bar seats are among the best walk-in dining in the city.

name: Keens Steakhouse
description: Open since 1885 with 90,000 clay churchwarden pipes lining the ceilings, Keens is the most storied steakhouse in New York. The mutton chop -- a massive prime cut served since opening day -- is the signature dish you cannot get anywhere else.

name: Rao's
description: The most exclusive table in New York since 1896. With only ten tables and no public reservations -- regulars own their nights -- Rao's is more private club than restaurant. The meatballs and lemon chicken are legendary, if you can get in.

name: Roberta's
description: The wood-fired pizzeria that put Bushwick on the dining map, featuring ingredients from a rooftop garden and a radio station in the back. Roberta's hipster-industrial vibe and exceptional Neapolitan pies have made it a Brooklyn institution.

name: Di Fara Pizza
description: Dom DeMarco opened Di Fara in 1965 and hand-made every pizza for nearly six decades. Now run by his son Dominick Jr., this Midwood institution still makes one of the finest slices in New York -- fresh mozzarella, hand-snipped basil, olive oil finished.

name: Sylvia's
description: The Queen of Soul Food has anchored Lenox Avenue since 1962, serving fried chicken, collard greens, and cornbread to everyone from Harlem locals to presidents. Sunday Gospel Brunch is a quintessential New York experience.
address: 328 Malcolm X Blvd, New York NY 10027

name: Nom Wah Tea Parlor
awards: NYC Institution Since 1920, Oldest Dim Sum Parlor in NYC
description: The oldest continuously running restaurant in Manhattan's Chinatown, serving dim sum on iconic Doyers Street since 1920. Made-to-order dumplings, rice rolls, and turnip cakes in a beautifully preserved vintage parlor.

name: Nan Xiang Xiao Long Bao
description: The soup dumpling institution of Flushing, Michelin-recommended for nine straight years. Delicate xiao long bao with rich, savory broth are the must-order, served in a bustling dining room in the heart of NYC's greatest Chinatown.

name: Zero Otto Nove
description: Named after the area code of chef Roberto Paciullo's hometown of Salerno, this Arthur Avenue gem serves wood-fired Neapolitan pizza and handmade pasta in the heart of the Bronx's Little Italy. The most beloved restaurant on NYC's original Italian strip.
